content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN (N≥2)(N \ge 2)(N≥2).

Define f(A)=∑i=1N−1(Ai+Ai+1)f(A) = \sum_{i = 1}^{N - 1} (A_i + A_{i + 1})f(A)=∑i=1N−1​(Ai​+Ai+1​).

You are given QQQ queries, each with a single integer XXX. Determine if there exists a rearrangement of the array AAA such that f(A)=Xf(A) = Xf(A)=X.

If there exists a valid rearrangement, print it, else print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the size of the array, and the number of queries, respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next QQQ lines each contain a single integer, XXX, the parameter for this query.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ lines corresponding to each query.


For the ithi^{th}ith query, if there doesn't exist a valid rearrangement of array AAA such that f(A)=Xf(A) = Xf(A)=X, print −1-1−1. Otherwise, let BBB denote the rearranged array AAA. Print NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.


If multiple arrays are possible, you may print any.
